recaps.text: In the first book, three children moved to a country cottage and found the Enchanted Wood, and in it the towering Faraway Tree. Living in its trunk were Moon-Face, with his slippery-slip slide, the elf Silky, the clanking Saucepan Man, the peevish Angry Pixie and Dame Washalot with her tipped-out wash-water. At the top, a hole in a cloud opened onto lands that changed from time to time - some delightful, some dangerous, and always to be left before they moved away. The children made firm friends of the tree folk and came safely home from their adventures.

The children's cousin comes to stay and refuses to believe their stories about a magic tree. Taken into the Enchanted Wood, he soon meets Moon-Face, Silky and the other folk for himself and is quickly won over. He learns the rules of the tree - which neighbours to avoid and how the land at the top keeps changing - as the children prepare for fresh adventures above the clouds.

With their cousin now a believer, the children climb to a series of new lands at the top of the tree, some full of fun and others best escaped quickly. They enjoy the tree folk's treats and help one another out of the usual scrapes, always mindful that the land overhead will eventually swing away and strand anyone who lingers too long.

After more visits to the changing lands - and a few narrow escapes when a land threatens to carry them off - the cousin has become as fond of the Faraway Tree as the children themselves. The adventures end with the friends safe once more, and the magic of the tree firmly proven to the newcomer.
